pi_items.verified_value: NREL 5MW Reference Turbine for Offshore System Development (NREL/TP-500-38060, 2009)

pi_items.metadata_value: Not explicitly stated, but ground truth implies CP approaches zero at certain TSR values
pi_items.verified_value: CP_max = 0.482 at TSR = 7.55 with pitch angle = 0°
pi_items.source: NREL/TP-500-38060 and multiple NREL forum discussions

pi_items.metadata_value: Implied by ground_truth having different TSR ranges for different pitch angles
pi_items.verified_value: CP is indeed a function of both Tip Speed Ratio (TSR) and pitch angle (Beta). CP varies significantly across these parameters.
pi_items.source: NREL forums, NREL/TP-500-38060, multiple aerodynamic analysis papers

pi_items.metadata_value: Implied: higher pitch angle reduces runaway TSR (7-12 @ 5deg vs 11-16 @ 0deg)
pi_items.verified_value: Pitch control (blade pitch adjustment) is the primary aerodynamic braking mechanism. Higher pitch angles shift the power coefficient curve and reduce power extraction. This effect is well-established in wind turbine control literature.
pi_items.source: Multiple sources: NREL control system papers, wind turbine braking system documentation, pitch control literature

pi_items.verified_value: QBlade is a validated wind turbine design and simulation software capable of rotor BEM (Blade Element Momentum) simulations for coefficient of power (CP) analysis
pi_items.source: QBlade documentation, multiple research papers using QBlade for NREL 5MW analysis

pi_items.metadata_value: Task requires running BEM simulation over TSR range 0-20
pi_items.verified_value: QBlade includes Rotor BEM submodule that can conduct rotor blade simulations over specified ranges of tip speed ratios (TSRs)
pi_items.source: QBlade Documentation 2.0.9.6, research papers on QBlade usage

privileged_info_summary: The task involves analyzing the aerodynamic braking effectiveness of the NREL 5MW reference turbine (a real offshore wind turbine model defined in NREL/TP-500-38060) using QBlade, a validated wind turbine simulation software. The NREL 5MW turbine has a maximum power coefficient of approximately 0.482 at TSR=7.55 with 0° pitch angle. Power coefficient (CP) is a function of both tip speed ratio (TSR) and pitch angle (Beta), and pitch control is the primary aerodynamic braking mechanism. The task requires identifying the TSR values where power coefficient drops to zero (runaway TSR) at 0° and 5° pitch angles. The ground truth values (TSR ranges of 11.0-16.0 at 0° pitch and 7.0-12.0 at 5° pitch) represent plausible post-stall region behavior where higher pitch angles shift the CP curve, requiring lower TSR values for zero power generation. However, these specific numeric ranges could not be independently verified from published sources and appear to be task-specific values, likely generated from QBlade simulations. The data is synthetic (no actual QBlade installation present), and the task simulates a realistic wind turbine analysis scenario.
